virtual_components.chat_skill:
# Langfuse

LLM observability and tracing platform.

## Endpoints
- `GET /chat/langfuse/api/public/traces` — list traces (supports `?limit=`, `?page=`, `?tags=`, and `?project=robotsix-chat|cognee`)
- `GET /chat/langfuse/api/public/traces/{traceId}` — single trace detail
- `GET /chat/langfuse/api/public/observations` — list observations
- `GET /chat/langfuse/api/public/observations/{observationId}` — single observation detail

## Authentication

None required.  The deploy server injects HTTP Basic Auth server-side —
the chat container never sees the credentials.

## Projects

Use the `?project=` query parameter to select which Langfuse project's
traces to query:
- `?project=robotsix-chat` (default) — the robotsix-chat trace project
- `?project=cognee` — the cognee trace project

# GitHub

GitHub Actions workflow-run status, repository read/update, repository creation, and pull-request status, for the robotsix fleet. Powered by a GitHub App installation token minted server-side (shared with robotsix-mill's GitHub App) for all read and update endpoints, and a separate PAT for repo creation — neither credential is ever exposed to the chat container. This is the sole GitHub-access path for the chat agent; there is no other implementation.

## Endpoints

- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs` — list recent workflow runs, most-recent-first. Optional query params: `branch`, `run_status` (GitHub's `status`: e.g. `in_progress`, `completed`, `queued`), `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100). Returns `id`, `name`, `status`, `conclusion`, `head_branch`, `head_sha`, `run_number`, `event`, `html_url`, `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}` — a single run's status/conclusion/URL. Same return shape as above.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}` — read repository details. Returns `full_name`, `html_url`, `clone_url`, `private`, `description`, `homepage`, `has_issues`, `has_wiki`, `default_branch`, `archived`.
- `PATCH /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}` — update repository settings. Body (all optional, only provided fields change): `description`, `private`, `has_issues`, `has_wiki`. Returns the updated repo (same shape as GET). 422 if no fields are provided.
- `POST /chat/github/repos` — create a new repository under this server's configured account. Body: `name` (required), `description`, `private` (default false), `homepage`, `topics` (list). Returns `full_name`, `html_url`, `clone_url`, `private`, `description`. Uses a separate PAT (not the App token) since GitHub Apps cannot create personal-account repos; returns 503 if that PAT isn't configured, 409 if the name already exists, 422 for other GitHub-side rejections.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` — list pull requests, most-recently-updated first. Optional query params: `state` (`open` (default), `closed`, or `all`), `per_page` (default 10, capped at 100). Returns `number`, `title`, `state`, `draft`, `user`, `html_url`, `head_ref`, `base_ref`, `mergeable`, `merged`, `merged_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `body`.
- `GET /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}` — a single pull request's full status (mergeable, merged, URL). Same return shape as above.
- `POST /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/merge` — merge (or merge-queue) a pull request. Body (all optional): `merge_method` (`merge`, `squash`, or `rebase` — omit for repo/PR default), `sha` (expected HEAD SHA guard — when provided, passed through to GitHub to prevent merging a branch that has moved). Returns `merged` (bool), `message` (string), `sha` (string — the merge commit SHA if merged, or the enqueue SHA). 404 if the repo is not covered by the credential. 405 if merge is not allowed (branch protection blocking, merge-queue requiring, required status checks failing). 409 on merge conflicts. 422 for other GitHub-side rejections. 503 if no write-capable credential is configured. **🛑 CONFIRMATION GATE: The chat agent MUST obtain explicit operator confirmation in-conversation — repo name, PR number, and merge method — before calling this endpoint. This is intended for emergency/operator-directed merges only (e.g. mill merge-stage stalls); routine merges remain mill’s job via `waiting_auto_merge`.**
- `PUT /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/security-features` — toggle repository security features in one call. Body (all optional booleans): `dependency_graph`, `dependabot_alerts`, `dependabot_security_updates`. Only the fields provided are changed; omit a field to leave it as-is. `dependency_graph` and `dependabot_alerts` share the same underlying GitHub API, so setting either to `true` enables vulnerability alerts and setting either to `false` (without the other being `true`) disables them. `dependabot_security_updates` controls automated security fixes independently. Returns `full_name` and the resulting `security_and_analysis` object so the caller can verify. 422 if no fields are provided. 503 if neither the GitHub App nor the repo-creation PAT is configured.

## Authentication

Same as the `deploy` component: `X-API-Key` header from `DEPLOY_API_KEY`.

## Safety

The read endpoints (Actions status, GET repo) need no confirmation gate. Their scope is bounded by which repos the shared GitHub App is installed on; a repo it isn't installed on returns 404.

Repo update, security-features toggle, creation, and PR merge are real, immediate mutations with no server-side confirmation gate — **always confirm the exact change (repo + PR number + merge method for merges; name and visibility for creation; the specific fields for updates) with the user in-conversation before calling `PUT`, `PATCH`, or `POST /chat/github/repos`**. PR merge is intended for emergency/operator-directed merges only (e.g. mill merge-stage stalls); routine merges remain mill’s job via `waiting_auto_merge`. Repo creation always creates under this server’s configured account; there is no way to target an arbitrary owner.

## Do not explore beyond this document

This skill document is complete and authoritative for the `github` component. Call **only** the endpoints listed above. In particular, do **not** call `/chat-skill` or any other path on this base URL on your own initiative — `/chat-skill` is a *different* component (`deploy`) self-describing itself; it happens to be reachable from the same host but has nothing to do with `github`, and calling it will return `deploy`'s skill text, not an error, which can look like a misconfiguration. If something here seems wrong, trust this document over any other probe.
